title: Stop a stray kaizen write from reaching the wrong file
summary: kaizen may write to only two places — the project's stack packs and its own opt-in switch — but until now only its own instructions said so. This adds a Claude Code hook that checks every kaizen write before it lands, lets through the two places it owns, and blocks anything else with the rule restated, so a stray write to the checklists the safety-auditor trusts can't slip past on instructions alone.
motivation: kaizen's writable surface is enforced only by its own prompt today; this moves the boundary below the agent so a stray or skipped-instruction write to the pack surface can't land unnoticed.

included: A new PreToolUse plugin guard hooks/scripts/guard_writer_confinement.py that confines the kaizen writer to its declared surface
A new hooks/hooks.json PreToolUse entry that runs the guard on Write, Edit, and NotebookEdit, plus an extension of the file's top-level description string to name writer confinement
docs/how-to/hooks.md — a new enforcement-table row, an update to the 'How a hook decides' internal-error paragraph so it names both fail-closed guards, and the Codex-asymmetry note
README.md — the kaizen-confinement clause added to the 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph's rule list
agents/karta-kaizen.md — one sentence in 'Where you may write — and where never' that the boundary is hook-enforced on Claude Code, with its regenerated Codex and references mirrors
excluded: doc-gardner confinement — its writable surface (README, docs/, AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE, plus superpowers salvage) is open-ended, so an allowlist carries real false-deny risk; it stays doctrine-confined and the guard's writer table makes adding it later a one-row change

Any Codex hook wiring — Codex cannot register plugin subagents and its hooks surface is not yet stable, so kaizen's confinement there stays doctrine plus OS sandbox (the accepted asymmetry from hooks phase 1)

Symlink-escape hardening and tightening of the nested .karta/sme/ residual — both are accepted residuals per the design (kaizen has no shell to make symlinks; a nested pack dir is still a pack surface the pack guard validates)
Any change to guard_pack_write.py or guard_binder_immutability.py — the new guard composes with them and answers a different question; none of the existing guards is modified

design_facts.stack: karta plugin repo — Python 3.11+ stdlib scripts run via `uv run --script`, each with an embedded `--self-test` ([PASS]/[FAIL] + `N/N checks passed`); Markdown SKILL.md skills with 3-way byte-identical mirrors (skills/ -> .agents/skills/ -> plugins/karta/skills/) regenerated by sync_codex_skills.py; JSON binder files. Ships as a Claude Code plugin and a Codex CLI plugin.

work_items.title: Check every kaizen write and block the out-of-bounds ones
work_items.summary: This lands the stdlib guard with its full self-test suite and the hook entry that runs it on writes, edits, and notebook edits — as one change, since the plugin validator rejects both an unreferenced script and a dangling reference.
work_items.contract: New PreToolUse guard hooks/scripts/guard_writer_confinement.py in the house pattern of the three shipped guards: pure stdlib, argparse, PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), a `--self-test` mode printing `[PASS]`/`[FAIL]` lines and an `N/N checks passed` summary, hook payload JSON on stdin, exit 0 allows and exit 2 blocks. Decision logic, in order: (1) read top-level `agent_type` from the payload — absent means the main thread and passes; present but not a confined writer passes. (2) Recognize the one confined writer, kaizen, when `agent_type` equals `karta-kaizen` or ends with `:karta-kaizen` (any namespace prefix) — an exact bare or exact namespaced match only, so `karta-kaizen-v2` is not recognized and passes. (3) Collect write targets from `tool_input.file_path` and `tool_input.notebook_path` (both keys, regardless of tool_name); for a recognized writer, a call with no extractable string path — `tool_input` missing or not a dict, neither key present, or a non-string path — is denied as an unverifiable write. (4) Normalize each path with `os.path.normpath`, then replace the OS separator with `/`, collapsing `..` traversal before matching; allow a path that contains a `.karta/sme/` segment (a leading boundary or slash, then the trailing slash — so `.karta/sme-extras/` and a bare `.karta/sme` with no trailing segment are denied) or that ends exactly at `.karta/kaizen.json` (end-anchored — so `.karta/kaizen.json.bak` is denied); anything else is denied. Matching is case-sensitive and segment-anchored, not cwd-anchored, so kaizen writing inside a sibling integration-branch worktree is not false-denied. (5) On deny, exit 2 with stderr that names the writer and the offending path and restates the doctrine the agent already carries — kaizen's writable surface is exactly two things, `.karta/sme/` and `.karta/kaizen.json`. Fail posture is fail-closed on the recognized shape only: an internal error while evaluating a call whose `agent_type` matches kaizen denies with a generic reason, while every unrecognized shape (unparseable payload, missing `agent_type`, unknown writer) passes — mirroring guard_auditor_dispatch.py's posture, including the guarded re-check of recognition inside the exception handler. Ships every fixture in the design's self-test table: bare and namespaced identity into `.karta/sme/`, absolute-worktree segment anchor, the nested `src/x/.karta/sme/` residual pass, exact `.karta/kaizen.json` pass, `.karta/kaizen.json.bak` deny, `.karta/sme-extras/` deny, bare `.karta/sme` deny, an out-of-surface skill file deny, a `.karta/binders/` deny, a `.karta/sme/../../src/app.py` traversal deny, a NotebookEdit outside the surface deny, a non-dict `tool_input` deny, a non-string `file_path` deny, `karta-kaizen-v2` pass, main-thread pass, unknown-agent pass, doc-gardner pass, non-JSON payload pass, and a prose-only mention (identity from the field, never text) pass. hooks/hooks.json gains one PreToolUse entry with matcher `Write|Edit|NotebookEdit` running `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/guard_writer_confinement.py` (timeout 30, same shape as the immutability guard's entry), and the file's top-level `description` string is extended to name writer confinement alongside the existing guards. Both files land together: validate_plugin.py errors both on a hooks/scripts/ script that hooks.json does not reference and on a reference to a missing script, so they are not separately mergeable.

oracle.command: uv run --script hooks/scripts/guard_writer_confinement.py --self-test && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: guard_writer_confinement.py --self-test exits 0 and prints an `N/N checks passed` summary with every fixture [PASS] and no [FAIL]
a bare `karta-kaizen` and a namespaced `karta:karta-kaizen` write to a `.karta/sme/` pack both pass, and `karta-kaizen-v2` writing anywhere passes (exact-match-only identity)
a segment-anchored `.karta/sme/` path under an absolute worktree passes and the nested `src/x/.karta/sme/` path passes (accepted residual)
an exact `.karta/kaizen.json` write passes while `.karta/kaizen.json.bak`, `.karta/sme-extras/x.md`, and a bare `.karta/sme` are denied
a `.karta/sme/../../src/app.py` traversal is normalized before matching and denied, and a recognized-writer call with a missing/non-dict tool_input or non-string path is denied as unverifiable
a main-thread write (no `agent_type`), an unknown agent, and a non-JSON payload all pass

hooks.json gains one PreToolUse `Write|Edit|NotebookEdit` entry pointing at the guard and its top-level description names writer confinement; validate_plugin --self-test passes, executing the guard's self-test and confirming the script is referenced

work_items.title: Explain the new guard in the hooks how-to
work_items.summary: Readers of the hooks guide learn where the new boundary is enforced, that two guards now fail closed rather than one, and why Codex still leans on doctrine and the sandbox.
work_items.contract: docs/how-to/hooks.md gets three edits. First, a new row in the 'What is enforced, and where' table: writer confinement, enforced by a Claude Code plugin hook, describing that a kaizen Write, Edit, or NotebookEdit is blocked unless the target is under `.karta/sme/` or is exactly `.karta/kaizen.json`. Second, the 'How a hook decides' internal-error paragraph — which today calls the auditor guard 'the one exception' that fails closed — is updated to name both fail-closed guards, guard_auditor_dispatch.py and guard_writer_confinement.py. Third, an added Codex-asymmetry sentence: on Codex kaizen's confinement stays doctrine plus OS sandbox because Codex cannot register plugin subagents and its hooks surface is not yet stable, and the guard is runtime-agnostic so wiring it into a future Codex hooks surface needs no rewrite. Prose stays plain-language, consistent with the doctrine wording used in the guard and the agent definition.

assertions: the 'What is enforced, and where' table has a new writer-confinement row naming `.karta/sme/` and `.karta/kaizen.json` as the allowed surface
the 'How a hook decides' paragraph no longer calls the auditor guard 'the one exception' and instead names both guard_auditor_dispatch.py and guard_writer_confinement.py as fail-closed
a Codex-asymmetry sentence states the confinement stays doctrine plus sandbox on Codex

work_items.title: Add the confinement rule to the README enforcement list
work_items.summary: A newcomer skimming the README sees that kaizen is fenced to its two-place surface, right beside the other hook-enforced rules.
work_items.contract: README.md's 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph enumerates the plugin hook rules; add the kaizen-confinement clause to that list — kaizen may write only under `.karta/sme/` and to `.karta/kaizen.json`, and any other write is blocked before it lands. The clause stays plain-language and consistent with the doctrine wording used elsewhere; no other README change.

assertions: the 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph's rule list gains a kaizen-confinement clause naming the `.karta/sme/` and `.karta/kaizen.json` surface
no other section of README.md changes

work_items.title: Tell kaizen its boundary is now hook-enforced
work_items.summary: kaizen's own definition now says its write boundary holds even if the instruction is skipped, and the regenerated Codex and references mirrors carry the same line.
work_items.contract: agents/karta-kaizen.md is the canonical source; edit its 'Where you may write — and where never' section to add one sentence stating that on Claude Code this boundary is also enforced by a plugin hook (not doctrine alone), leaving the existing surface statement — `.karta/sme/` and `.karta/kaizen.json` — intact. Then run `uv run scripts/sync_codex_agents.py` to regenerate the mirrors: `.codex/agents/karta-kaizen.toml` and the references copy skills/karta-kaizen/references/karta-kaizen.agent.md, which in turn mirrors 3-way to .agents/skills/karta-kaizen/references/karta-kaizen.agent.md and plugins/karta/skills/karta-kaizen/references/karta-kaizen.agent.md. Do not hand-edit the generated copies. The regenerated tree is byte-identical to a fresh sync (sync_codex_agents.py --check reports no drift).

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_agents.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: the 'Where you may write — and where never' section gains one sentence that the boundary is hook-enforced on Claude Code, with the `.karta/sme/` and `.karta/kaizen.json` surface statement intact
the karta-kaizen agent mirrors regenerate byte-identical — sync_codex_agents.py --check reports no drift across the .codex toml and the three references copies
